description.task: Implement a SpringBootDockerfileGenerator class that programmatically generates an optimized multi-stage Dockerfile for a Spring Boot application. The output should use a build stage with Maven and a lean runtime stage, following best practices for layer caching.
instructions: The runtime base image should be 'eclipse-temurin:<javaVersion>-jre-alpine' for a lean image
Add 'EXPOSE <serverPort>' after COPY in the runtime stage
When enableHealthCheck=true, add: 'HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -f http://localhost:<port>/actuator/health || exit 1'
Add 'USER 1001' before ENTRYPOINT to run as non-root
description.hint: Append each line individually with sb.append(...). For HEALTHCHECK: sb.append('HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -f http://localhost:' + config.serverPort() + '/actuator/health || exit 1\n'). For USER: sb.append('USER 1001\n').
description.expectedOutput:
# Build stage
FROM maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-17 AS build
WORKDIR /build
COPY pom.xml .
RUN mvn dependency:go-offline -q
COPY src ./src
RUN mvn package -DskipTests -q

# Runtime stage
FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=build /build/target/*.jar app.jar
EXPOSE 8080
HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/actuator/health || exit 1
USER 1001
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

description.task: Implement a DockerComposeResolver class that models docker-compose service dependencies (depends_on). Given service definitions and their dependencies, compute the start-up order and identify circular dependencies.
instructions: addService() registers a service with zero or more dependencies
dfs() implements post-order DFS: mark inProgress on entry, recurse into each dependency, add to order on exit, mark visited
dfs() throws IllegalStateException('Circular dependency: ' + service) when encountering a service already in inProgress
startupOrder() ensures dependencies appear before dependent services (topological sort)
findCircularDependencies() returns empty list when no cycles exist
description.hint: In dfs(): inProgress.add(service); for (String dep : services.getOrDefault(service, List.of())) { if (inProgress.contains(dep)) throw new IllegalStateException(...); if (!visited.contains(dep)) dfs(dep, ...); } order.add(service); inProgress.remove(service); visited.add(service);
description.expectedOutput:
Startup order: [database, redis, app, nginx]
Cycles: []

description.task: Create an EnvVarInjector class that simulates container environment variable injection. It merges variables from multiple sources (Dockerfile defaults, docker-compose env, runtime overrides) with a defined precedence order. Higher priority sources override lower ones.
instructions: addEnv() merges the given vars into the source-specific map using putAll
resolve() iterates over sources from lowest to highest priority, calling putAll on the result
RUNTIME_OVERRIDE has highest priority and overrides COMPOSE_ENV and DOCKERFILE_DEFAULT
COMPOSE_ENV overrides DOCKERFILE_DEFAULT but is overridden by RUNTIME_OVERRIDE
get() and hasKey() call resolve() internally — no need to duplicate the merge logic
description.hint: The resolve() implementation is already provided in the skeleton. The key insight is that putAll() on a LinkedHashMap overwrites existing keys, so later sources override earlier ones. Just ensure addEnv() correctly merges into the source map.
description.expectedOutput:
DB_HOST: postgres
LOG_LEVEL: WARN
Total vars: 4
